codeReview.summary: OpenAIBackend implementation is functionally correct and complete. The chat() method properly sends to OpenAI API via POST /chat/completions, returns LlmResponse with content, tool_calls, and usage tracking. health_check() works correctly. All 3 OpenAI tests pass. However, the handoff references commit f6ed1d2 which is actually the MCP client implementation in swell-tools, not the OpenAI backend - the OpenAI backend was implemented in commit 3a8c017 'feat: complete Rust backend skeleton'.

issues.description: Handoff references wrong commit. commitId 'f6ed1d2' is 'feat(swell-tools): implement MCP client for external tool servers', not the OpenAI backend. The OpenAI backend was actually implemented in commit '3a8c017 feat: complete Rust backend skeleton' and modified in e5ada74.

Test coverage is limited. Tests only verify model name and role conversion - they don't test: (1) API error handling paths (non-2xx responses), (2) JSON parse failure handling, (3) actual API communication, (4) tool_calls parsing. However, the implementation does handle these cases properly in code.

The finish_reason field in ResponseChoice is deserialized but never used. Marked with #[allow(dead_code)]. This is acceptable since finish_reason is useful for debugging but not required for core functionality.

tool_calls parsing uses unwrap_or with empty object for malformed arguments - this silently swallows JSON parse errors. This is intentional for graceful handling but could hide bugs in tool argument serialization.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature specifies skillName 'rust-worker' but this skill doesn't exist. The actual skill is at .factory/skills/worker/SKILL.md. Worker invoked 'Rust' skill instead and completed successfully.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json has skillName: 'rust-worker' but only .factory/skills/worker/SKILL.md exists. Transcript shows worker used 'Rust' skill.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Implementation follows correct Rust conventions (async_trait, tracing, proper error handling with SwellError::LlmError). The AGENTS.md documents these conventions. No issues found.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Implementation uses async_trait, tracing::{debug, warn}, proper Result return types.

summary: Implementation is functionally correct with all tests passing. Blocking issue: handoff references wrong commit (f6ed1d2 instead of 3a8c017). Non-blocking: limited test coverage for error paths, but implementation handles them correctly. Worker verified pre-existing implementation rather than creating new code.
